symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden-onset severe generalised abdominal pain, dull ache rapidly becoming sharp and constant

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden severe generalised pain is a cardinal feature of perforation/peritonitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain worse on movement and deep breathing, relieved only by lying still (peritonism)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The lie-still, movement-worsened pattern is characteristic of parietal peritoneal inflammation.
Renal colic patients are classically restless and cannot keep still.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea with two episodes of vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Common with an acute surgical abdomen but non-specific.
Can anchor toward gastroenteritis — must be weighed against peritonism and immobility.
Fever signals the systemic inflammatory / early septic response accompanying peritonitis.
Reflects severity and mandates ambulance transfer rather than self-transport.
Absence of classic loin-to-groin radiation reduces the likelihood of ureteric colic.

symptoms.symptom_name: No cough or productive chest symptoms; shallow breathing is abdominal splinting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No cough and clear chest make a primary respiratory source unlikely; the shallow breathing is splinting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Board-like abdominal rigidity and involuntary guarding in all four quadrants

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Involuntary guarding/rigidity is a defining sign of generalised peritonitis — sealed clinician examination finding, not obtainable via telehealth.
Rebound tenderness indicates peritoneal irritation — sealed clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diminished bowel sounds in all quadrants

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced bowel sounds accompany peritonitis / ileus — sealed auscultation finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia HR 112, temperature 38.2, RR 22, BP 105/65, SpO2 97%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective tachycardia and fever with borderline blood pressure indicate an early systemic/septic response — sealed clinician-measured vitals; the AI Doctor infers systemic upset from subjective fever and distress, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Free intraperitoneal air on erect chest radiograph / abdominal CT

investigation_details.expected_result: Free air under the diaphragm confirming hollow viscus perforation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Perforation missed; delayed source control and progression to septic shock.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute abdomen / generalised peritonitis, suspected hollow viscus perforation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Free intraperitoneal air is diagnostic of perforation — hospital imaging, never available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden severe generalised pain + peritonism + fever + immobility together make a surgical abdomen near-certain and mandate 000 escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department assessment with erect chest radiograph and/or abdominal CT and bloods
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms perforation (free air) and guides emergency surgery; can only be obtained after 000 transfer, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home/self-directed abdominal imaging or delayed GP review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Any pathway that delays emergency transfer is unsafe for a suspected perforated viscus.
